title: podman docker swagger mismatches
body:
I'm running podman 2.1.1 and tried using it with a client generated from vendor/github.com/docker/docker/api/swagger.yaml.

These differences came up:

Command is not a string, but an array of strings.
```
        "Command": [
            "/bin/bash"
        ],
```

ports don't contain required PrivatePort and Type:
```
        "Ports": [
            {
                "hostPort": 3000,
                "containerPort": 3000,
                "protocol": "tcp",
                "hostIP": ""
            }
```

mounts is not an array of type Mount, but of strings.
```
        "Mounts": [
            "/root"
        ],
```

comments.body: To be clear, you're testing the Docker swagger against our Docker-compat endpoints? Can you clarify what endpoint in particular this is happening with?

This is against the `/containers/json` endpoint on `systemctl` `--user` `podman.socket`.

I'm using the docker swagger file. I tried using the https://storage.googleapis.com/libpod-master-releases/swagger-latest-master.yaml but the tool I'm using has an issue with it.
@baude @jwhonce PTAL
@tmds Which tool are you using? We've put a lot of work in the swagger  support and I am sorry to hear you are having issues.
@jwhonce I'm using NSwag to generate a C# client. It may be a limitation of the tool, rather than an issue with the podman swagger file. I'll cc you on the issue in the NSwag repo.

@jwhonce this is what I need to change to the swagger file to make NSwag happy:
```diff
diff --git a/swagger-latest-master.yaml b/swagger-latest-master.yaml
index d9b199f..1891a02 100644
--- a/swagger-latest-master.yaml
+++ b/swagger-latest-master.yaml
@@ -10300,7 +10300,8 @@ paths:
       - application/json
       responses:
         "200":
-          $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
+          schema:
+            $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
         "400":
           $ref: '#/responses/BadParamError'
         "404":
@@ -10329,7 +10330,8 @@ paths:
       - application/json
       responses:
         "200":
-          $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
+          schema:
+            $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
         "404":
           $ref: '#/responses/NoSuchManifest'
         "409":
@@ -10383,7 +10385,8 @@ paths:
       - application/json
       responses:
         "200":
-          $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
+          schema:
+            $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
         "400":
           $ref: '#/responses/BadParamError'
         "404":
@@ -10415,7 +10418,8 @@ paths:
       - application/json
       responses:
         "200":
-          $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
+          schema:
+            $ref: '#/definitions/IDResponse'
         "400":
           $ref: '#/responses/BadParamError'
         "404":
@@ -10811,7 +10815,8 @@ paths:
       - application/json
       responses:
         "200":
-          $ref: '#/definitions/IdResponse'
+          schema:
+            $ref: '#/definitions/IdResponse'
         "400":
           $ref: '#/responses/BadParamError'
         "500":

```

`IdResponse` is also used with the `schema` in the docker swagger file.

Note: the podman swagger has both a `IdResponse` and `IDResponse`definition.
@tmds Thanks for the details!

@jwhonce another issue the tool has with podman swagger:
```
  /images/{name:.*}:
    delete:
      description: Delete an image from local storage
      operationId: removeImage
      parameters:
      - description: name or ID of image to delete
        in: path
        name: name:.*
        required: true
        type: string
```
What is this `:.*` for?

I'm trying to use the API to do the same things as the Docker API _Run a container_ example: https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/sdk/examples/#run-a-container

I'm running into these issues with the podman swagger:
- ContainerCreate is missing the body parameter
- ContainerWait seems to accept different values for condition
- ContainerLogs doesn't have a response 200 return type

And, for the tool to pick up the type names I need to move the `title` properties under the schema (like it is in the docker swagger file). For example:
```diff
@@ -11392,8 +11392,8 @@ responses:
       type: object
   ContainerCreateResponse:
     description: Create container
-    title: "ContainerCreateResponse"
     schema:
+      title: "ContainerCreateResponse"
       properties:
         Id:
           description: ID of the container created
```
The `operationId` gets used by code generators (as mentioned in the swagger spec). It would be nice if these would match between docker swagger and podman swagger. Currently these ids are different.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@tmds Could you open a PR with podman to make the changes you want.  That would make this go through a lot faster then relying on other engineers to work the PR.
Thanks.

@rhatdan how does the swagger get generated? Is it from the comments in the code, like this one?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3fcf346890c0437611fc18c30d58cc2d9f61fe6c/pkg/api/server/register_containers.go#L12-L34
yup

@baude does this comment 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/c3b39842ce76e3e3bfc843abfebeeb603f04591d/pkg/api/handlers/compat/swagger.go#L9-L16

cause generation of:

```yaml
  ContainerCreateResponse:
    description: Create container
    schema:
      properties:
        Id:
          description: ID of the container created
          type: string
          x-go-name: ID
        Warnings:
          description: Warnings during container creation
          items:
            type: string
          type: array
      type: object
```
What would be a way to add a `title: ContainerCreateResponse` for such types under the `schema` property?
@baude see my previous question: What generates the swagger for these types? And can we make it include a `title` property?
@jwhonce ptal
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> What generates the swagger for these types? And can we make it include a title property?

ping @jwhonce

@jwhonce @mheon @baude my requests here and https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9126, https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9123, https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9121 have been idle for a few weeks. Can you take a look? I think these are real issues when using the podman swagger file.

The REST API may be usable, but the swagger needs love.

I'm going to try use the docker swagger file instead.

@jwhonce thanks for working on this issue past week!

The only thing stopping me now from using the podman swagger is https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9126.
In particular:
- the missing parameter for the build context on the build image command, and
- the missing parameter for configuration on the create container command.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhonce are we good?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> swagger:response

@tmds The go-swagger tool currently does not allow us to set the title key on responses.

> `IdResponse` is also used with the `schema` in the docker swagger file.
> 
> Note: the podman swagger has both a `IdResponse` and `IDResponse`definition.

go-swagger picks up IdResponse from vendor'ed code. I changed all our definitions to use IDResponse.
